intro:
There is no information on a plant called Jacmaia incana. However, I found information on the plant Jacquinia incana, also known as Jacmaia. 

Jacquinia incana is a plant species from the Jacquinia genus, native to tropical regions of Central and South America, and is used as a medicinal plant. The health benefits of Jacquinia incana include its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties, which have been traditionally used to treat various ailments such as wounds, fever, and respiratory issues.

The bioactive constituents of Jacquinia incana include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. These constituents have been shown to possess antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory activities, contributing to the plant's therapeutic effects.

The most common herbal preparations made with Jacquinia incana include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of health conditions, from skin irritations to respiratory infections.
